generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor generating torque (τ_M) and angular velocity (ω) based on input current (I_M) within the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox component in the first axis that transmits torque (τ_G) and angular velocity (ω) from motor to output shaft with gear reduction."
]

instance GearWheel : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Intermediate gearwheel component in the first axis transmission chain that facilitates torque (τ_GW) transfer between motor and cycloidal gearbox."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the motor shaft measuring motor output angle (r_M) for differential analysis of elasticity and friction."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderData
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind the gearbox measuring output angle (r_out) for backlash reduction and positional uncertainty mitigation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderData
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring input current (I_M) to the motor, converted to torque (τ_M) using manufacturer-provided motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData
]

instance SinumerikController : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller providing real-time access to position, speed, acceleration, and reference values for the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ControllerData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance SecondaryEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance ControllerData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SinumerikController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLBlockDefinition : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) describing structural architecture of first axis components (Motor, Gearbox) and their interconnections."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance SysMLInternalBlock : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) modeling rotational and current flows between Motor and Gearbox components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance IdealGearboxModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Base Simulink model representing ideal gearbox behavior without friction effects (Figure 4a)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box Simulink implementation of Coulomb-Viscous friction model for gearbox friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box Simulink implementation of Stribeck friction model accounting for stiction and breakaway effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box Simulink implementation of Dahl friction model for dynamic friction characterization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box Simulink implementation of LuGre friction model for detailed friction dynamics simulation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance MotorOutputSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific Simulink model simulating motor output (Simulation M) including friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance GearWheelOutputSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific Simulink model simulating gearwheel output (Simulation GW) with friction compensation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance GearboxOutputSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Overall system output simulation model (Simulation GB) incorporating all friction models."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance MeasuredMotorOutput : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw measured motor output angle (r_M) from motor encoder with time-stamped metadata."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance MeasuredOutputAngle : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw measured output angle (r_out) from secondary encoder with positional uncertainty reduction."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance MeasuredInputCurrent : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw measured input current (I_M) converted to torque (τ_M) using motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance ControllerReferenceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Reference values (target/actual position, velocity, acceleration) from SINUMERIK controller."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ControllerData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance SimulatedMotorTorque : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed motor torque (τ̂_M) from friction models accounting for static/kinetic/viscous friction components."
    DTDFVocab:fromData IdealGearboxModel, CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedGearTorque : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed gear torque (τ̂_G) incorporating all friction models in transmission chain."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearWheelOutputSimulation, GearboxOutputSimulation
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedOutputTorque : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Final simulated output torque (τ̂_out) after all friction compensations applied."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxOutputSimulation
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML 1.7 tool enabling structural and behavioral modeling of first axis components and their interconnections."
    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralModelingService, BehavioralModelingService
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink environment for implementing executable friction models and component-specific simulations."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionSimulationService, ComponentSimulationService
]

instance MQTTEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol enabling real-time streaming of sensor data (I_M, r_M, r_out) from SINUMERIK controller."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamingService
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing components computing derived traces (torque, velocity) from raw sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataDerivationService, FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unified dashboard displaying data traces and their relationships for real-time analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualAnalyticsService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell framework contextualizing data traces and enabling semantic linking between physical components and virtual models."
    DTDFVocab:enables ContextualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance StructuralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing SysML Block Definition Diagrams for structural representation of first axis components."
    DTDFVocab:provides StructuralInsight
]

instance BehavioralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing SysML Internal Block Diagrams for behavioral analysis of component interactions."
    DTDFVocab:provides BehavioralInsight
]

instance FrictionSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing white-box and black-box friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) in Simulink."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionAnalysisInsight, ModelUpdateAction
]

instance ComponentSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing component-specific simulations (Motor, Gearwheel, Gearbox) with friction compensation."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentPerformanceInsight
]

instance DataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service streaming real-time sensor data (I_M, r_M, r_out) via MQTT protocol from physical system."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceInsight
]

instance DataDerivationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service computing derived data traces (torque, velocity) from raw sensor measurements."
    DTDFVocab:provides DerivedDataInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service identifying static, kinetic, and viscous friction components in gearbox transmission."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionDeviationInsight
]

instance VisualAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service displaying torque-velocity relationships and friction-related deviations in unified dashboard."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualAnalyticsInsight
]

instance ContextualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service linking physical components to virtual models using Asset Administration Shell framework."
    DTDFVocab:provides ContextualInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance StructuralInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural representation of first axis components (Motor, Gearbox) and their interconnections via SysML BDDs."
]

instance BehavioralInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Behavioral analysis of rotational and current flows between components using SysML IBDs."
]

instance FrictionAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time identification of static, kinetic, and viscous friction components affecting motion accuracy."
]

instance ModelUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of friction models based on discrepancies between simulated and reference data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ComponentPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Performance analysis of individual components (Motor, Gearwheel, Gearbox) under friction conditions."
]

instance DataTraceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Time-stamped data traces (I_M, r_M, r_out) with traceable origins from physical system."
]

instance DerivedDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Computed derived data (torque, velocity) from raw sensor measurements with processing metadata."
]

instance FrictionDeviationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative analysis of friction-related deviations impacting motion accuracy and energy efficiency."
]

instance VisualAnalyticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Qualitative and quantitative visualization of torque-velocity relationships for debugging and optimization."
]

instance ContextualInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Semantic linking between physical components and virtual models through Asset Administration Shell."
]

instance OperationalOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommendations for preventive maintenance or control strategy adjustments based on wear patterns."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DynamicModelExchangeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time swapping of friction models (white-box/black-box) to adapt to operational changes (wear, tooling)."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C18: Environment
instance FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Environment containing all components and models related to the first vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 robot."
    base:contains FirstAxisComponent, DataProcessingComponent, VisualizationComponent
]

// C19: Components
instance FirstAxisComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "First vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 robot comprising permanent magnet synchronous motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox."
    base:isContainedIn FirstAxisEnvironment
]

instance DataProcessingComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Processing pipeline computing derived data traces from raw sensor measurements."
    base:isContainedIn FirstAxisEnvironment
]

instance VisualizationComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Unified dashboard component displaying real-time friction analysis and system performance."
    base:isContainedIn FirstAxisEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates at **real-time or near-real-time scales**, synchronized with the PT’s operational frequency. Key time-scale details include:
   - **Sensor data acquisition**: Continuous streaming via MQTT (latency not explicitly stated but implied to be low for real-time control).
   - **Model computation**: Simulation models (MATLAB Simulink) execute dynamically to process incoming data traces (e.g., I_M) and generate outputs (e.g., ˆr_M) with **millisecond-level granularity** (assumed based on industrial robot control systems).
   - **Dashboard updates**: Visualization occurs in real-time to reflect live data and model outputs, enabling immediate analysis of friction-related deviations."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is organized as follows:
   - **Centralized DS**: A single Digital Shadow instance focuses on the **first axis** of the MABI MAX 100 robot, integrating **engineering models (SysML)**, **simulation models (Simulink)**, and **live data traces** (MQTT-streamed sensor data).
   - **Data Flow**:
     1. **Physical System (PT)**: Generates raw data (e.g., I_M, encoder readings) and reference values (position, velocity).
     2. **Processing Component**: Consumes input data traces (e.g., I_M) and executes simulation models (e.g., friction models) to produce output data traces (e.g., ˆr_M).
     3. **Digital Shadow**: Aggregates and abstracts data traces, linking them to their sources (e.g., sensors, models) via metadata.
   - **Orchestration**: The DS acts as a **passive intermediary**, enabling real-time analysis of friction but **not autonomous control** of the PT. The Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller remains the primary control authority."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT covers the **as-operated phase** of the physical system’s lifecycle, focusing on **real-time monitoring and analysis** of the first axis during active machining operations. It does not explicitly address the **as-designed** (ideal) or **as-manufactured** phases, though engineering models (SysML) provide structural and behavioral descriptions that could inform these stages. The DT’s purpose is **operational optimization**, particularly for improving motion accuracy via friction modeling."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
   - **Model Validation**: Comparison of **simulated data** (e.g., ˆr_M) against **reference data** (e.g., r_M) to quantify discrepancies caused by friction or other dynamic effects.
   - **Uncertainty Quantification**: The DS methodology supports traceability of data points to their sources (e.g., sensors, models), enabling stakeholders to assess uncertainty in friction estimates.
   - **Verification Mechanisms**:
     - **SysML models**: Structurally verify the physical system’s architecture (e.g., component interconnections).
     - **Simulink simulations**: Validate friction models (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous) against empirical data to ensure computational fidelity.
   - **Error Handling**: The DS framework allows for **real-time model swapping**, enabling adaptive correction of friction estimates as operational conditions change (e.g., wear)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the PT and DT involves:
   - **Network Protocol**: **MQTT** for real-time streaming of sensor data (e.g., I_M, output angle) from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller to the DT.
   - **Data Format**: Time-stamped data traces (e.g., torque τ, angular velocity ω) organized into arrays of floating-point values, with metadata linking traces to their sources (e.g., sensor channels).
   - **Synchronization**: Implicit real-time synchronization between PT and DT, as the DS processes live data to compute friction-related outputs (e.g., ˆr_M) with millisecond-level granularity (assumed)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is **hosted locally** (likely on the same network as the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller) to ensure low-latency processing of MQTT-streamed data. Deployment specifics are not detailed, but the use of **MATLAB Simulink** and **SysML** suggests a **software-based deployment** on a dedicated workstation or embedded system capable of real-time simulation. The dashboard for visualization is also part of this deployment."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DT does not explicitly describe horizontal integration with external information systems beyond the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller** and **MQTT-based data streaming**. However, the **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework (implied in the methodology) could theoretically enable integration with other digital systems (e.g., ERP, MES) for broader contextual data sharing."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following standards:
   - **Modeling Standards**:
     - **SysML (OMG Standard)**: For structural and behavioral modeling of the physical system.
     - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Implied as part of the Digital Shadow methodology for semantic interoperability (ISO/IEC 4000-2).
   - **Data Exchange**:
     - **MQTT Protocol**: Industry-standard for lightweight, publish-subscribe messaging in IoT/industrial applications.
   - **Simulation Standards**:
     - **MATLAB Simulink**: Widely used for dynamic system modeling and simulation in industrial applications."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The document does not explicitly address **cybersecurity risks** (e.g., data encryption, authentication) or **safety considerations** (e.g., remote operation hazards). However:
   - The **one-way data flow** (Digital Shadow paradigm) inherently reduces risks of unauthorized PT intervention by the DT.
   - **MQTT security**: While not detailed, MQTT typically supports TLS for secure data transmission, which could be implied for industrial use cases.
   - **Safety**: The DT is designed for **monitoring and analysis only**; no remote control or autonomous operation of the PT is described, mitigating direct safety risks."
]
